content.json:
# 🛡️ Azure Arc Compliance, Security & Governance

## **Your Complete Hybrid Infrastructure Compliance Dashboard**

**🎯 What This Demonstrates**: How Azure Arc transforms compliance, security, and governance for hybrid infrastructure using real Azure Monitor data.

**✨ What You'll See:**
- **📊 Estate & Scope** - Complete inventory of Arc-enabled servers with connectivity health
- **🔒 Policy Compliance** - NIST CSF v2.0 compliance status and policy violations
- **🔧 Patch Posture** - Update Manager security patch status and risk assessment
- **👤 Privileged Activity** - Essential Eight admin logon monitoring and privileged access tracking
- **🔐 Protocol Hardening** - TLS and secure communication compliance reporting
- **🚨 Security Alerts** - Defender for Cloud threat intelligence and alert analysis
- **⚙️ Configuration Drift** - Change tracking and configuration governance

**🚀 Why This Matters**: This dashboard demonstrates how Azure Arc enables enterprise-grade compliance reporting across your entire hybrid estate with unified visibility, policy enforcement, and security monitoring.

**💡 Demo Value**: Shows practical implementation of compliance frameworks (NIST CSF v2.0, Essential Eight) using real Azure data sources, providing actionable insights for governance, risk, and compliance teams.

---

### 📋 **Global Filters & Settings**

parameters.name: LogAnalyticsWorkspace
parameters.label: 📊 Log Analytics Workspace
parameters.query:
resources
| where type =~ 'microsoft.operationalinsights/workspaces'
| project id, name, resourceGroup, subscriptionId
| order by name asc

content.json:
## 📊 **Arc-Enabled Server Inventory**

**What You're Looking At**: Complete inventory of Azure Arc-enabled servers across your hybrid infrastructure.

**Why This Matters**: Understand the scope and scale of your Arc deployment, verify connectivity status, and identify infrastructure patterns.

**Compliance Value**: This data forms the foundation for all compliance reporting - you can't secure what you can't see.

content.query:
// Arc Server Summary KPIs
resources
| where type =~ 'microsoft.hybridcompute/machines'
| extend 
    osType = tostring(properties.osName),
    status = tostring(properties.status),
    lastStatusChange = todatetime(properties.lastStatusChange),
    agentVersion = tostring(properties.agentVersion)
| extend 
    daysOffline = case(
        status == "Connected", 0,
        status == "Disconnected", datetime_diff('day', now(), lastStatusChange),
        datetime_diff('day', now(), lastStatusChange)
    )
| summarize 
    TotalServers = count(),
    ConnectedServers = countif(status == "Connected"),
    DisconnectedServers = countif(status == "Disconnected"),
    WindowsServers = countif(osType has "Windows"),
    LinuxServers = countif(osType has "Linux")
| extend 
    ConnectivityHealth = round(todouble(ConnectedServers) * 100.0 / todouble(TotalServers), 0),
    HealthyMachinesText = strcat(ConnectedServers, " of ", TotalServers, " Arc machines online")
| project 
    ['Connectivity Health'] = strcat(tostring(ConnectivityHealth), "%"),
    ['Status Summary'] = HealthyMachinesText,
    ['Total Servers'] = TotalServers,
    ['Connected'] = ConnectedServers,
    ['Disconnected'] = DisconnectedServers

// Detailed Arc Server Inventory Grid
resources
| where type =~ 'microsoft.hybridcompute/machines'
| extend 
    machineName = name,
    osType = tostring(properties.osName),
    osVersion = tostring(properties.osVersion),
    status = tostring(properties.status),
    lastStatusChange = todatetime(properties.lastStatusChange),
    agentVersion = tostring(properties.agentVersion),
    domainName = tostring(properties.domainName)
| extend 
    daysOffline = case(
        status == "Connected", 0,
        status == "Disconnected", datetime_diff('day', now(), lastStatusChange),
        datetime_diff('day', now(), lastStatusChange)
    )
| extend 
    healthStatus = case(
        status == "Connected", "🟢 Healthy",
        status == "Disconnected" and daysOffline <= 1, "🟡 Recent Disconnect",
        status == "Disconnected" and daysOffline <= 7, "🟠 Short Disconnect",
        status == "Disconnected", "🔴 Extended Offline",
        "⚪ Unknown"
    )
| project 
    ['Machine Name'] = machineName,
    ['Health Status'] = healthStatus,
    ['OS Type'] = osType,
    ['OS Version'] = osVersion,
    ['Agent Version'] = agentVersion,
    ['Domain'] = domainName,
    ['Location'] = location,
    ['Resource Group'] = resourceGroup,
    ['Days Offline'] = daysOffline,
    ['Last Status Change'] = lastStatusChange
| order by ['Health Status'] desc, ['Machine Name'] asc
content.title: 🖥️ Arc Server Inventory Details

content.json:
## 🔒 **Policy Compliance Status**

**What You're Looking At**: Compliance status for security initiatives and policies, with focus on NIST CSF v2.0 framework.

**Why This Matters**: Policy compliance is the foundation of governance - understand where you meet standards and where gaps exist.

**Compliance Value**: Demonstrates adherence to regulatory frameworks like NIST CSF v2.0, ISO 27001, and other security standards essential for audit and certification.

content.query:
// Overall Policy Compliance for Arc Servers
PolicyResources
| where type =~ 'microsoft.policyinsights/policystates'
| extend 
    complianceState = tostring(properties.complianceState),
    resourceType = tostring(properties.resourceType)
| extend 
    isArcServer = resourceType has 'Microsoft.HybridCompute/machines'
| where isArcServer == true
| summarize 
    Total = count(),
    Compliant = countif(complianceState == 'Compliant'),
    NonCompliant = countif(complianceState == 'NonCompliant'),
    Exempt = countif(complianceState == 'Exempt')
| extend 
    ComplianceRate = round(todouble(Compliant) * 100.0 / todouble(Total), 0),
    NonComplianceRate = round(todouble(NonCompliant) * 100.0 / todouble(Total), 0)
| extend 
    ComplianceStatus = case(
        ComplianceRate >= 95, "🟢 EXCELLENT",
        ComplianceRate >= 85, "🟡 GOOD",
        ComplianceRate >= 70, "🟠 NEEDS ATTENTION",
        "🔴 CRITICAL"
    )
| project 
    ['Compliance Status'] = ComplianceStatus,
    ['Compliance Rate %'] = strcat(tostring(ComplianceRate), "%"),
    ['Total Policies'] = Total,
    ['Compliant'] = Compliant,
    ['Non-Compliant'] = NonCompliant,
    ['Non-Compliant Description'] = strcat(tostring(NonCompliant), " policies failing")

// Top Failing Policies on Arc Servers
PolicyResources
| where type =~ 'microsoft.policyinsights/policystates'
| extend 
    complianceState = tostring(properties.complianceState),
    resourceType = tostring(properties.resourceType),
    policyDefId = tostring(properties.policyDefinitionId),
    policySetDefId = tostring(properties.policySetDefinitionId),
    policyCategory = coalesce(
        tostring(properties.policyDefinitionCategory),
        tostring(properties.policySetDefinitionCategory),
        "General"
    ),
    resourceId = tostring(properties.resourceId)
| extend 
    isArcServer = resourceType has 'Microsoft.HybridCompute/machines'
| where isArcServer == true and complianceState == 'NonCompliant'
| extend 
    machineName = tostring(split(resourceId, '/')[8]),
    severity = case(
        policyCategory in ('Security', 'Network Security'), 'Critical',
        policyCategory in ('Monitoring', 'Backup'), 'High',
        'Medium'
    ),
    // Try to get display name first, then extract from ID
    policyDisplayName = coalesce(
        tostring(properties.policySetDefinitionDisplayName),
        tostring(properties.policyDefinitionDisplayName),
        ""
    ),
    policyGuid = case(
        isnotempty(policySetDefId), tostring(split(policySetDefId, '/')[4]),
        isnotempty(policyDefId), tostring(split(policyDefId, '/')[4]),
        "unknown"
    )
| extend 
    // Create meaningful name from display name or known GUID mappings
    policyName = case(
        isnotempty(policyDisplayName), policyDisplayName,
        // Map known policy initiative GUIDs to readable names
        policyGuid == "184a0e05-7b06-4a68-bbbe-13b8353bc613", "NIST CSF v2.0",
        policyGuid == "89c6cddc-1c73-4ac1-b19c-54d1a15a42f2", "NIST Cybersecurity Framework",
        policyGuid == "1f3afdf9-d0c9-4c3d-847f-89da613e70a8", "Azure Security Benchmark",
        policyGuid == "4f9dc7db-30c1-420c-b61a-e1d640128d26", "CIS Microsoft Azure Foundations Benchmark",
        policyGuid == "cf25b9c1-bd23-4eb6-bd5c-f4f3ac644a5f", "Essential Eight Initiative",
        // If it's an initiative but no known mapping, use generic name
        isnotempty(policySetDefId), strcat("Policy Initiative: ", substring(policyGuid, 0, 8)),
        // For individual policies, use the GUID substring
        strcat("Policy: ", substring(policyGuid, 0, 8))
    )
| extend 
    // Clean up common Azure policy naming patterns
    cleanPolicyName = case(
        policyName has "deploy-", replace_string(policyName, "deploy-", "Deploy "),
        policyName has "audit-", replace_string(policyName, "audit-", "Audit "),
        policyName has "require-", replace_string(policyName, "require-", "Require "),
        policyName has "deny-", replace_string(policyName, "deny-", "Deny "),
        policyName has "append-", replace_string(policyName, "append-", "Append "),
        // Handle NIST CSF initiative
        policyName has "nist", "NIST Cybersecurity Framework",
        policyName has "cis", "CIS Microsoft Azure Foundations Benchmark",
        policyName has "azure-security", "Azure Security Benchmark",
        // Truncate if still too long
        strlen(policyName) > 60, strcat(substring(policyName, 0, 57), "..."),
        policyName
    )
| summarize 
    AffectedMachines = dcount(machineName),
    TotalViolations = count(),
    SamplePolicyId = take_any(policyDefId)
    by cleanPolicyName, policyCategory, severity
| project 
    ['Policy Name'] = cleanPolicyName,
    ['Category'] = policyCategory,
    ['Severity'] = severity,
    ['Affected Arc Servers'] = AffectedMachines,
    ['Total Violations'] = TotalViolations,
    ['Sample Policy ID'] = SamplePolicyId
| order by ['Affected Arc Servers'] desc
content.title: 🚨 Top Policy Violations on Arc Servers

content.json:
## 🔧 **Patch Management & Security Posture**

**What You're Looking At**: Estimated patch risk assessment based on Azure Arc server connectivity health and status patterns.

**Why This Matters**: Unpatched systems are the #1 attack vector for ransomware and breaches. While this shows estimated exposure based on server health, it helps identify servers likely needing attention.

**Compliance Value**: Demonstrates proactive patch risk monitoring approach for compliance frameworks requiring timely security updates (ISO 27001, NIST CSF, Essential Eight). Note: These are estimates - deploy Azure Update Manager for precise patch data.

content.query:
// Patch Security Scorecard for Arc Servers - Aligned with Table Logic
// Use same health scoring logic as detailed table for consistency
resources
| where type =~ 'microsoft.hybridcompute/machines'
| extend 
    status = tostring(properties.status),
    osType = tostring(properties.osName),
    lastHeartbeat = todatetime(properties.lastStatusChange)
| extend 
    daysOffline = datetime_diff('day', now(), lastHeartbeat)
| extend 
    HealthScore = case(
        daysOffline <= 0, 95,                                  // Recently active (regardless of status)
        status == "Connected" and daysOffline <= 1, 90,
        status == "Connected" and daysOffline <= 7, 70,
        status == "Connected", 50,
        status == "Disconnected" and daysOffline <= 7, 30,
        10
    )
| extend 
    EstimatedCritical = case(
        HealthScore >= 90, 0,
        HealthScore <= 30, 8,
        HealthScore <= 50, 3,
        HealthScore <= 70, 1,
        0
    )
| summarize 
    TotalMachines = count(),
    ExcellentHealth = countif(HealthScore >= 90),
    CriticallyExposed = countif(EstimatedCritical > 0),
    TotalCriticalPatches = sum(EstimatedCritical)
| extend 
    SecurityPosture = round(todouble(ExcellentHealth) * 100.0 / todouble(TotalMachines), 0),
    CriticalExposure = round(todouble(CriticallyExposed) * 100.0 / todouble(TotalMachines), 0)
| extend 
    PostureStatus = case(
        CriticallyExposed == 0, "🟢 EXCELLENT",
        SecurityPosture >= 75, "🟡 GOOD",
        SecurityPosture >= 60, "🟠 NEEDS WORK",
        "🔴 CRITICAL"
    )
| project 
    ['Security Posture'] = PostureStatus,
    ['Fully Patched %'] = strcat(tostring(SecurityPosture), "%"),
    ['Critical Exposure %'] = strcat(tostring(CriticalExposure), "%"),
    ['Machines w/ Critical'] = CriticallyExposed,
    ['Total Critical Patches'] = TotalCriticalPatches,
    ['Total Security Patches'] = TotalCriticalPatches,
    ['Critical Description'] = strcat(tostring(CriticallyExposed), " machines need attention"),
    ['Data Source'] = "Arc Health Assessment"

// Arc Servers Requiring Critical Patching - Simplified Approach
// Get Arc servers and estimate patch risk based on health status
resources
| where type =~ 'microsoft.hybridcompute/machines'
| extend 
    machineName = name,
    OS = tostring(properties.osName),
    status = tostring(properties.status),
    lastHeartbeat = todatetime(properties.lastStatusChange),
    agentVersion = tostring(properties.agentVersion)
| extend 
    daysOffline = datetime_diff('day', now(), lastHeartbeat)
| extend 
    HealthScore = case(
        daysOffline <= 0, 95,                                  // Recently active (regardless of status)
        status == "Connected" and daysOffline <= 1, 90,
        status == "Connected" and daysOffline <= 7, 70,
        status == "Connected", 50,
        status == "Disconnected" and daysOffline <= 7, 30,
        10
    )
| extend 
    EstimatedCritical = case(
        HealthScore >= 90, 0,
        HealthScore <= 30, 8,
        HealthScore <= 50, 3,
        HealthScore <= 70, 1,
        0
    ),
    EstimatedSecurity = case(
        HealthScore >= 90, 0,
        HealthScore <= 30, 5,
        HealthScore <= 50, 2,
        HealthScore <= 70, 1,
        0
    )
| extend 
    RiskLevel = case(
        EstimatedCritical >= 5, "🔴 Critical",
        EstimatedCritical >= 3, "🟠 High",
        EstimatedCritical >= 1, "🟡 Elevated",
        EstimatedSecurity >= 3, "🟡 Moderate",
        "🟢 Low"
    )
| where EstimatedCritical > 0 or EstimatedSecurity > 0
| project 
    ['Machine Name'] = machineName,
    ['Risk Level'] = RiskLevel,
    ['OS Type'] = OS,
    ['Critical Patches'] = EstimatedCritical,
    ['Security Patches'] = EstimatedSecurity,
    ['Total Updates'] = EstimatedCritical + EstimatedSecurity,
    ['Resource Group'] = resourceGroup,
    ['Data Source'] = "Arc Health Assessment",
    ['Days Offline'] = daysOffline
| order by ['Critical Patches'] desc, ['Security Patches'] desc
content.title: 🚨 Arc Servers Requiring Critical Patching

content.json:
## 🚨 **Security Monitoring & Threat Intelligence**

**What You're Looking At**: Combined view of security alerts, privileged activity, and security events from your Arc-enabled servers.

**Why This Matters**: Real-time security monitoring is essential for detecting and responding to threats across your hybrid infrastructure.

**Compliance Value**: Demonstrates continuous security monitoring required by frameworks like Essential Eight, NIST CSF, and ISO 27001.

content.query:
// Security Events Summary - Cross-Platform Log Analytics Data
// Windows: Event table for security events via DCR/AMA
// Linux: Syslog table for authentication and security events
let WindowsSecurityEvents = Event
| where TimeGenerated >= ago(30d)
| where EventLog in ("Security", "System", "Application")
| where EventID in (4625, 4648, 4672, 4720, 4728, 4732, 1102, 4697, 7045) // Security events
| extend 
    EventType = case(
        EventID == 4625, "Failed Logon",
        EventID == 4648, "Explicit Credentials", 
        EventID == 4672, "Privileged Logon",
        EventID == 4720, "Account Created",
        EventID == 4728, "Member Added to Global Group",
        EventID == 4732, "Member Added to Local Group",
        EventID == 1102, "Audit Log Cleared",
        EventID == 4697, "Service Installed",
        EventID == 7045, "Service Installed",
        "Other Security"
    ),
    Severity = case(
        EventID in (4672, 4648, 1102), "High",
        EventID in (4625, 4720, 4697, 7045), "Medium",
        "Low"
    ),
    Platform = "Windows"
| summarize EventCount = count() by Computer, EventType, Severity, Platform;
let LinuxSecurityEvents = Syslog
| where TimeGenerated >= ago(30d)
| where Facility in ("auth", "authpriv", "security", "daemon") 
| where SyslogMessage has_any("sudo", "su:", "failed", "authentication", "login", "ssh", "denied", "refused")
| extend 
    EventType = case(
        SyslogMessage has "failed" and SyslogMessage has "login", "Failed Logon",
        SyslogMessage has "sudo", "Privileged Command",
        SyslogMessage has "su:" and SyslogMessage has "session", "Privilege Escalation",
        SyslogMessage has "useradd" or SyslogMessage has "user added", "Account Created",
        SyslogMessage has "ssh" and SyslogMessage has "failed", "SSH Failed",
        SyslogMessage has "denied" or SyslogMessage has "refused", "Access Denied",
        "Security Event"
    ),
    Severity = case(
        SyslogMessage has "sudo" or SyslogMessage has "su:", "High",
        SyslogMessage has "failed" or SyslogMessage has "denied", "Medium",
        "Low"
    ),
    Platform = "Linux"
| summarize EventCount = count() by Computer, EventType, Severity, Platform;
union WindowsSecurityEvents, LinuxSecurityEvents
| summarize 
    TotalEvents = sum(EventCount),
    HighSeverityEvents = sumif(EventCount, Severity == "High"),
    MediumSeverityEvents = sumif(EventCount, Severity == "Medium"),
    LowSeverityEvents = sumif(EventCount, Severity == "Low"),
    WindowsServers = dcountif(Computer, Platform == "Windows"),
    LinuxServers = dcountif(Computer, Platform == "Linux")
| extend 
    EventRate = round(todouble(TotalEvents) / 30.0, 1),
    HighRiskExposure = case(TotalEvents > 0, round(todouble(HighSeverityEvents) * 100.0 / todouble(TotalEvents), 1), 0.0),
    SecurityStatus = case(
        HighSeverityEvents == 0, "🟢 LOW RISK",
        HighSeverityEvents <= 5, "🟡 MODERATE",
        HighSeverityEvents <= 20, "🟠 ELEVATED",
        "🔴 HIGH RISK"
    )
| project 
    ['Security Status'] = SecurityStatus,
    ['Total Events'] = strcat(tostring(TotalEvents), " events (30 days)"),
    ['High Risk Events'] = strcat(tostring(HighSeverityEvents), " high severity"),
    ['Event Rate'] = strcat(tostring(EventRate), " events/day"),
    ['Platform Coverage'] = strcat(tostring(WindowsServers), " Windows + ", tostring(LinuxServers), " Linux"),
    ['Risk Exposure %'] = strcat(tostring(HighRiskExposure), "%")

// Privileged Activity Monitoring - Fixed Schema
// Based on schema investigation: Event table uses RenderedDescription for user extraction
let WindowsPrivileged = Event
| where TimeGenerated >= ago(30d)
| where EventLog == "Security" and EventID == 4672
| project TimeGenerated, Computer, EventID, RenderedDescription
| extend User = trim(" ", extract(@"Account Name:\s+([^\s]+)", 1, RenderedDescription))
| extend IsPrivileged = 1
| project TimeGenerated, Computer, User, Activity = "Privileged Logon", IsPrivileged;
let LinuxPrivileged = Syslog
| where TimeGenerated >= ago(30d)
| where Facility in ("auth", "authpriv") 
| where SyslogMessage contains "sudo" or SyslogMessage contains "su " or SyslogMessage contains "session opened for user root"
| project TimeGenerated, Computer, SyslogMessage
| extend User = case(
    SyslogMessage contains "session opened for user root", "root",
    SyslogMessage contains "sudo", extract(@"for user ([^\s]+)", 1, SyslogMessage),
    SyslogMessage contains "su ", extract(@"su to ([^\s]+)", 1, SyslogMessage),
    "unknown"
)
| extend IsPrivileged = 1
| project TimeGenerated, Computer, User, Activity = "Privileged Access", IsPrivileged;
// Combine and summarize
union WindowsPrivileged, LinuxPrivileged
| where isnotempty(User) and User != "unknown" and User != "SYSTEM"
| summarize 
    PrivilegedActivities = sum(IsPrivileged),
    UniqueUsers = dcount(User),
    LastActivity = max(TimeGenerated)
    by Computer
| extend RiskLevel = case(
    PrivilegedActivities > 50, "🔴 High Risk",
    PrivilegedActivities > 10, "🟠 Medium Risk", 
    "🟢 Low Risk"
)
| project 
    ['Server Name'] = Computer, 
    ['Privileged Activities'] = PrivilegedActivities, 
    ['Unique Users'] = UniqueUsers, 
    ['Last Activity'] = LastActivity, 
    ['Risk Level'] = RiskLevel
| order by ['Privileged Activities'] desc
content.title: 👤 Cross-Platform Privileged Access Activity (Essential Eight)

content.query:
// Configuration Drift Detection - Fixed Field Resolution
// Monitor machine connectivity and system changes via DCR-collected data
let ConnectivityHealth = Heartbeat
| where TimeGenerated >= ago(30d)
| summarize 
    LastHeartbeat = max(TimeGenerated),
    HeartbeatCount = count(),
    FirstHeartbeat = min(TimeGenerated)
    by Computer
| extend 
    DaysOffline = datetime_diff('day', now(), LastHeartbeat),
    ConnectivityDrift = case(
        datetime_diff('day', now(), LastHeartbeat) > 7, "🔴 Offline",
        datetime_diff('day', now(), LastHeartbeat) > 1, "🟠 Intermittent",
        HeartbeatCount < 1000, "🟡 Degraded",
        "🟢 Healthy"
    );
let SystemChanges = Event
| where TimeGenerated >= ago(30d)
| where EventLog in ("System", "Application") 
| where EventID in (7045, 7036, 1102, 4697, 6005, 6006) // Service changes, system events
| extend 
    ChangeType = case(
        EventID == 7045, "Service Installation",
        EventID == 7036, "Service State Change",
        EventID == 1102, "Audit Log Cleared",
        EventID == 4697, "Service Installed",
        EventID == 6005, "Event Log Started",
        EventID == 6006, "Event Log Stopped",
        "System Change"
    ),
    Severity = case(
        EventID in (1102, 4697, 7045), "High",
        EventID in (6005, 6006), "Medium",
        "Low"
    )
| summarize 
    SystemChanges = count(),
    HighRiskChanges = countif(Severity == "High"),
    MediumRiskChanges = countif(Severity == "Medium"),
    LastChange = max(TimeGenerated),
    ChangeTypes = make_set(ChangeType, 5)
    by Computer;
// Use left join and explicitly handle field resolution
ConnectivityHealth
| join kind=leftouter SystemChanges on Computer
| extend 
    TotalChanges = coalesce(SystemChanges, 0),
    HighRiskChangesCount = coalesce(HighRiskChanges, 0),
    MediumRiskChangesCount = coalesce(MediumRiskChanges, 0),
    // Recalculate DaysOffline to avoid field resolution issues
    DaysOfflineCalc = datetime_diff('day', now(), LastHeartbeat)
| extend 
    ChangeVelocity = case(
        HighRiskChangesCount >= 10, "🔴 High Activity",
        HighRiskChangesCount >= 5, "🟠 Moderate Activity",
        TotalChanges >= 10, "🟡 Low Activity",
        "🟢 Minimal Activity"
    ),
    DailyChangeRate = round(todouble(TotalChanges) / 30.0, 1)
| project 
    ['Server Name'] = Computer,
    ['Change Velocity'] = ChangeVelocity,
    ['Total Changes'] = TotalChanges,
    ['High Risk Changes'] = HighRiskChangesCount,
    ['Medium Risk Changes'] = MediumRiskChangesCount,
    ['Connectivity Status'] = ConnectivityDrift,
    ['Days Offline'] = DaysOfflineCalc,
    ['Daily Rate'] = DailyChangeRate,
    ['Heartbeat Count'] = HeartbeatCount
| order by ['Total Changes'] desc
content.title: ⚙️ Configuration Change Analysis
